data.title: Qualcomm declares $0.89 dividend
data.description:
• Qualcomm (QCOM) declares $0.89/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.21%
  • Payable March 26; for shareholders of record March 5; ex-div March 5.
  • See QCOM Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: HP and Logitech get rating cuts, while Garmin upgraded at Barclays
data.description:
Barclays downgraded ratings of HP (HPQ) and Logitech International (LOGI), while upgrading Garmin (GRMN).

HP (HPQ)

The firm downgraded HP to Underweight from Equal weight and lowered the price target to $18 from $24.

The analysts believe secular challenges across both personal computers, or PCs, and Printing, combined with a lack of catalyst, will cause the stock to remain pressured in 2026. The analysts also expect the current memory cycle to pressure HP's PC business with further downside potential in the year.

The analysts said that although memory cycles are nothing new for HP, the degree and intensity of the current cycle could pressure units materially worse than prior cycles. Though near-term the business could see a pull-in bump from customers looking to secure now with known pricing versus later in the year, the analysts expect demand could degrade.

Barclays noted that the commercial business should be more insulated, though it could see pushouts. HP has enough memory inventory on hand for the first half with the second half more of an issue on both supply and pricing, according to the analysts.

In addition, Barclays said that the Windows 11 refresh is largely done in the U.S. with the refresh now 60% captured, and it does not see much upside in the year. The analysts added that they expect there could be further margin degradation on both the gross and operating lines, given memory supply and competitive pressures.

Shares of HP fell about 2% premarket on Friday.

Garmin (GRMN)

Barclays upgraded Garmin to Equal weight from Underweight and raised the price target to $217 from $208. 

The analysts said that they are more positive on  Garmin given the undemanding valuation, diversification of the business, and continued strength in the wearables business (about 60% of total) that continues to benefit from positive secular trends around wellness.

The analysts view the company as more insulated from potential consumer contraction, given the more high-end, niche applications and hardware Garmin touts versus broader-user focused wearables.

Logitech International (LOGI)

Barclays firm cut Logitech's rating to Equal weight from Overweight and reduced the price target on the stock to $98 from $134.

The analysts said they are concerned about potential macro weakness with softening in the North America (almost 50% of total revenues) consumer market called out during the last earnings (largely gaming).

The analysts added that business-to-business, or B2B, remains less than 50% of the business, with about 60% of  Logitech's business B2C. Inflationary pressures and uneven macro could weigh on upside this year, though the analysts acknowledge the company’s focus on operational leverage.

Shares of Logitech dipped about 1% premarket on Friday.

data.title: TransDigm to buy two aerospace parts companies for combined $2.2B
data.description:
TransDigm (TDG) said Friday it agreed to acquire Jet Parts Engineering and Victor Sierra Aviation Holdings from Vance Street Capital for a combined ~$2.2B in cash.

Jet Parts Engineering designs and manufactures aerospace aftermarket solutions, primarily proprietary OEM-alternative parts and repairs, and it serves commercial, regional and cargo airline customers, as well as maintenance, repair and overhaul providers; nearly all of JPE's revenue comes from the commercial aftermarket.

Victor Sierra Aviation designs, manufactures and distributes proprietary PMA and other aftermarket parts serving the commercial aerospace end market, primarily the general aviation and business aviation sectors; nearly all of VSA's revenue comes from the commercial aftermarket.

"The companies' highly engineered, proprietary OEM-alternative parts and services generate nearly 100% commercial aftermarket revenue," TransDigm (TDG) CEO Mike Lisman said; the two companies generated a combined ~$280M in revenues in 2025.

data.title: Micron breaks ground on $100 billion New York Megafab Site
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) on Friday broke ground on its $100 billion memory manufacturing complex in Onondaga County, New York, and called it the largest semiconductor facility in the United States.

The chipmaker’s stock rose over 4% in premarket trading on Friday.

The facility is expected to generate 50,000 jobs in New York, while production is expected to start in 2030, Micron said.

“As the largest private investment in New York state history, Micron’s Central New York project will be home to the most advanced memory manufacturing in the world and will help meet the growing demands of AI systems and devices that are central to the modern economy,” the company said.

As part of the development, Micron plans to invest $250 million in workforce development, education, transportation, and housing through a community investment framework with Empire State Development.

Micron Chairman and CEO Sanjay Mehrotra was joined at the ceremony by officials from the Trump administration, New York state and local governments, among others.

Last year, Micron (MU) announced plans to expand its U.S. investments to around $150 billion in domestic memory manufacturing and $50 billion in R&D, creating an estimated 90,000 direct and indirect jobs.

The ~$200 billion broader U.S. expansion vision includes two high-volume fabs in Idaho, up to four high-volume fabs in New York, the expansion and modernization of its existing manufacturing fab in Virginia.

data.title: Cardiol Therapeutics announces $13.5M bought-deal private placement
data.description:
• Cardiol Therapeutics (CRDL) has entered into a bought-deal private placement with Canaccord Genuity for 10.38M units priced at $1.30/unit, raising gross proceeds of $13.5M.
  • The underwriter has an over-allotment option to purchase up to an additional 10% of units, potentially increasing proceeds by up to $1.35M.
  • Each unit includes one Class A common share and one-half of a common share purchase warrant.
  • Each whole warrant is exercisable at $1.75/share for a period of 24 months from issuance.
  • Proceeds will be used to advance research and clinical development programs and for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: KHC, NVO, NET, CART
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Friday, as upbeat bank earnings and a tech rally boosted market sentiment. Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

Cloudflare (NET) rebounded more than 1% in premarket trading as investors digested the company’s announcement of its intent to acquire Human Native. Shares of the internet infrastructure company fell more than 2% on Thursday following the initial disclosure of the deal.

Novo Nordisk (NVO) gained 3.71% before the opening bell after reports that its Wegovy pill garnered 3,071 U.S. prescriptions in the first four days of commercial rollout. The actual prescription count is likely higher, as the data only reflects retail prescriptions and excludes those filled through Novo’s direct-to-consumer platform, NovoCare Pharmacy.

Kraft Heinz (KHC) slipped 1.14% in premarket trade after Morgan Stanley downgraded the stock to Underweight from Equal-weight. The brokerage warned that the U.S. food sector faces rising competitive risk as value-focused pricing, promotions, and private label momentum reaccelerate into 2026.

Maplebear (CART) edged up 0.43% before the opening bell even as Wedbush Securities cautioned that the company, which operates under the Instacart name, could face negative impact from Uber Technologies’ expanded partnership with Kroger across its platform.

data.title: Lombard Street Capital renames to Lithium Africa, completes share consolidation
data.description:
• Lombard Street Capital (LSC.P:CA) has changed its name to Lithium Africa Corp., effective January 15, 2026.
  • The company completed a 24-for-1 share consolidation, with 24 pre-consolidation shares exchanged for one post-consolidation share.
  • The name change and consolidation are part of a business combination with Lithium Africa Resources Corp.
  • The business combination is expected to close in the coming weeks.

data.title: SRx Health invests in Opendoor's common stock
data.description:
SRx Health Solutions (SRXH) on Friday announced an investment in common stock of the digital platform for residential real estate transactions, Opendoor Technologies (OPEN).

SRXH shares were +1.10% pre-market to $0.19, while OPEN was +1.43% to $6.39.

Financial terms of the investment were not disclosed.

The investment was made as part of SRXH's broader capital allocation strategy.

SRx Health Solutions said it may increase or decrease its ownership position in Opendoor from time to time, and the investment does not impact its core operational focus.

data.title: Urban One approves 1-for-10 reverse stock split to regain Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Urban One (UONE) (Nasdaq: UONE and UONEK) said on Friday its board has approved a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of all classes of its common stock, including its publicly traded Class A and Class D shares.
  • The reverse split is intended to help the company regain compliance with Nasdaq’s $1.00 minimum bid price requirement for its Class D common stock.
  • It is expected to take effect at 11:59 p.m. on January 22, 2026, with split-adjusted trading to begin on January 23.
  • Under the reverse split, every 10 shares of Class A and Class D common stock will be converted into one share of the respective class.
  • Fractional shares will not be issued, with shareholders receiving cash in lieu based on the closing price on the effective date.
  • UONEK closed +5.38% at $0.875.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Zhibao Technology regains compliance with Nasdaq listing rules
data.description:
• Zhibao Technology (NASDAQ: ZBAO) said on Friday that it received a compliance notice from the Listing Qualifications Department of  Nasdaq on January 12, 2026.
  • The notice said the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250(c)(1) by filing its 2025 Annual Report with the SEC on January 9, 2026.
